merger_name: Bupa – Barings Australian Private Credit Fund

other_parties.name: Barings Australia Pty Ltd as trustee and manager of Barings Australian Private Credit Fund

merger_description:
Bupa HI Pty Ltd (**Bupa**) has entered into an agreement with Barings Australia Pty Ltd (**Barings**) under which Bupa will commit capital in exchange for units in Barings Australian Private Credit Fund (**BAPCF**) (the **Acquisition**).

Barings acts as both trustee and manager of BAPCF and is an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of Barings LLC, a global alternative asset manager headquartered in North Carolina.

BAPCF is an Australian unit trust and unregistered managed investment scheme, which raises and manages capital from wholesale investors and deploys that capital primarily into senior secured loans to privately held middle market companies connected to Australia and New Zealand.

Bupa is a wholly owned subsidiary of Bupa Group, an international healthcare group. In Australia, Bupa Group primarily offers health insurance, provides dental, optical and other clinical care networks, and operates residential aged‑care homes.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Bupa HI Pty Ltd (Bupa) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed agreement with Barings Australia Pty Ltd (Barings),
under which Bupa will commit capital in exchange for units in
Barings Australian Private Credit Fund (BAPCF), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Bupa, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Bupa Group, an
international healthcare group. In Australia, Bupa Group primarily
offers health insurance, provides dental, optical, and clinical care
networks, and operates residential aged care homes.
The vendor, Barings, acts as both trustee and manager of BAPCF
and is an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Barings LLC, a global
alternative asset manager headquartered in North Carolina.
The target, BAPCF, is an Australian unit trust and unregistered
managed investment scheme which raises and manages capital
from wholesale investors and deploys that capital primarily into
senior secured loans to privately held middle market companies
connected to Australia and New Zealand.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the goods and
services supplied by the parties in Australia
b. the Acquisition will not result in any substantive change in
the day-to-day operation or investment decisions of
BAPCF.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
